intro: Cieca verbascifolia, also known as Verbascum cieca, is a perennial plant species in the Scrophulariaceae family, characterized by its tall stem and large, white or yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Verbascum cieca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Verbascum cieca is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
